differential_diagnoses: Vitamin A deficiency (xerophthalmia and optic neuropathy)
Nutritional optic neuropathy (Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency)
next_steps: Obtain serum vitamin A level (retinol) and retinol-binding protein
Obtain serum vitamin B12, folate, copper, zinc, and methylmalonic acid levels
Urgent ophthalmology consultation for comprehensive dilated fundus examination and potentially electroretinography
Initiate high-dose vitamin A supplementation (typically oral or intramuscular based on severity)
Consult pediatric nutrition/dietetics for counseling on selective eating disorder management
Refer to pediatric neurology for consideration of underlying metabolic or genetic testing if nutritional screen is negative

differential_diagnoses: Vitamin A deficiency (Xerophthalmia and nutritional optic neuropathy)
Protein-energy malnutrition with associated vitamin/mineral deficiencies
Nutritional optic neuropathy (Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency)
next_steps: Obtain serum levels of vitamins A (retinol), D (25-hydroxyvitamin D), and C (ascorbic acid)
Order comprehensive metabolic panel, complete blood count, and iron studies
Urgent ophthalmology evaluation including dilated fundus exam and electroretinography (ERG)
Initiate appropriately dosed vitamin A and micronutrient supplementation
Consult pediatric nutrition and a feeding therapy specialist to manage ARFID
Perform skeletal survey or imaging to evaluate for signs of nutritional bone disease
